storyInfo: The time and place for the exchange ceremony have not yet been confirmed. The Astynomos called Lydia arranges for Titi to visit the site of the rebuilding of the First Temple.

attributes.content: I've been dreaming about the guerrillas again, Kassandra.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: This is the third time you've come to Endymion Temple to ask for a dream reading, Megas.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: You still dream of that night seven years ago?
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Yes...
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: We hid in the forest on that dark night. The Sargonians surrounded the wood, hunting for us.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: We could not make a sound, let alone risk a light. We had to disassemble our weapons in pitch darkness.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: Screws, springs, Originium components... I tied the parts of my Arts Unit to my clothes and shoes, disguised as accessories.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: The fear had me so cold that I did not notice when an Originium component had cut my palm open. I thought the wet stuff was sweat.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: When the sun rose, we walked past the Sargonians, disguised as lumberjacks from a nearby village, about a dozen in total. We'd be dead if they saw through our disguise.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: They sized us up. For a moment there, I thought the game was up. In my panic, I tripped over a tree root and plunged into the mud, face-first.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: A Sargonian laughed and rapped on my shoulder with the hilt of his sword, disdainfully. But I was happy. I knew we were safe.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: I keep dreaming about it. Do you think...?
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: You want to know if it's a divine revelation?
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Last year, I saw with my own eyes how the heroes spoke their will through you on the Agora.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: You are the speaker of the heroes of Minos, and the only priest in Athenius who can tell a divine revelation from a normal dream.
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: If the gods will that I take up arms again, then I will... no matter how much I fear...
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: Please sit down. Your legs are trembling and your face is pale.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: The heroes of Minos will not force their children back into a nightmare when they have already seen far too much of the horrors of war.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: You mean...?
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: I think you have misinterpreted the revelation.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Wisdom and courage shine upon you and your friends in place of the twin moons who closed their eyes, and delivered you in the morn, just as you woke up from dream to greet the beautiful Athenian dawn...
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: The virtues of the heroes should be praised, but the pain that has lingered for seven years has blinded you to the beautiful fruit that has ripened on the branches of fate...
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Pick it, for it is the heroes' blessing upon you.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: A blessing... on an ordinary Minoan like me?
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: Our gods and heroes were once ordinary Minoans.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Thank you, Kassandra. I'm beginning to understand...
attributes.name: Depressed Man

attributes.content: I have come to relieve you, Kassandra.
attributes.name: Acolyte

attributes.content: It's already time? So many visitors to the temple today.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: You have so much on your plate, not just with visitors to Endymion Temple, but also acting on behalf of the head priest of Kereseira's First Temple in administrative duties while the position remains vacant.
attributes.name: Acolyte

attributes.content: There's no need to worry about me, Folia. It is my calling to interpret revelation and speak on behalf of the gods.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: So, you should know better than I do that the man's dream was mundane, not a divine revelation. To use more Columbian words—
attributes.name: Acolyte

attributes.content: He may have post-traumatic stress disorder. I know.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: Why didn't you tell him?
attributes.name: Acolyte

attributes.content: Divine revelation should be an honor for any Minoan, but there were only shame and fear in his eyes.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: He wants to receive guidance from the gods, but he fears that the nightmare is truly a revelation that he cannot refuse.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: If he believes in my interpretation more than he believes in himself, then I have a responsibility... to make his life a little easier, if nothing else.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I'll prepare some herbal tea with a soothing effect for him, and ask a doctor to follow up.
attributes.name: Acolyte

attributes.content: You have the afternoon off, don't you? You should stop spending it on the temple and focus on yourself.
attributes.name: Acolyte

attributes.content: Thank you, Folia. You take so much weight off my shoulders.
attributes.name: Kassandra

attributes.content: I'm so very sorry, Hekádemos.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I dozed off as soon as I reached the hotel. I didn't realize I would make you wait for a full three hours...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: There's no need to worry, Kyría Mashayer. Waiting is part of the job for the Astynomos' Grammateús.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You still look weak. Are you sure you want to visit the First Temple right now? You can rest a little longer.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Yes, I'm sure! If anything will bring me back to full strength right now, it's Kereseira's First Temple!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I understand the original site of the temple was where Kereseira, the founding hero of Minos, first received divine revelation in the mountains.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: The first temple that she built there became the genesis of Minos.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: When Sargon... invaded Minos, they rebuilt the three great poleis as nomadic cities, but Kereseira's war-torn First Temple remained where it was.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Now, after a century, Athenius is rebuilding this temple, giving me the opportunity to see the historical building in its original form! This is incredibly exciting!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: You are surprisingly well-versed in Minoan history... and surprisingly honest.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Unfortunately, the original plans of the First Temple have been lost, and thus cannot be perfectly recreated.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: However, there's quite a bit of 'classical' tradition in the way that they're building the temple.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: What do you mean?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: 'To revive the Minoan spirit, we must emulate the heroes who built Athenius, and build the temple of our own accord.'
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Before the referendum took place, these words by the original proponent moved everyone's hearts, and the vote on rebuilding the temple passed almost unanimously.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: No engineering blueprint, no staffing. Kereseira's First Temple shall be built through the passion of the Minoan people, and the veneration of the heroes that flows in their veins.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Wow! A living example of the Minoan spontaneousness that has driven so much artistic achievement!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Thank you. I hope it's completed soon...
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Rebuilding in the classical style... how interesting! The proponent of the rebuilding must be an ambitious hero beloved by the people!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Uh... how can I describe that Forte lady?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: (Why is he taking so much time to think? Did you ask a question you shouldn't have?)
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: (Even if I did, I'm sure Hekádemos won't mind! He looks so nice, after all. Maybe he's choosing his words carefully, with a Minoan love of poetry.)
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Uh...
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Give me three seconds, Kyría Mashayer.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: O-Okay.
attributes.name: Titi
Hekádemos
attributes.content: Three, two, one...

attributes.name: Hekádemos
attributes.content: Alright!
You've thought of the answer?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: No, it's now five o'clock, and my shift is over.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Huh?
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I'll answer your question tomorrow, when I'm back at work. Enjoy your visit.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: But... we aren't even at the First Temple yet!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: See those scaffolds? This street is a stone's throw from the main entrance to the temple. I'm sure you can find your own way.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Wait—
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Now, if you'll excuse me, I have a drinking engagement with a friend. He'll kill me if I'm late. Goodbye.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You know, I was worried that the Astynomos' Grammateús would just take me around to see mundane tourist sites, leaving me with nothing to talk about when we get back.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: You've got a way of smoothing things over for people, you know that?
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: If you ask me, I think the deferential-looking Liberi kid just enjoyed a three-hour break!
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: Well, no need to complain. There's plenty of time. Let's check out the surrounding area.
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: This is the land of heroes, after all. Maybe we'll walk out the door and straight into a heroic epic!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: I knew it! You came looking for adventure!
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: Rats, I came to you when I left the Grand Bazaar because I wanted a sugar mommy with money. Little did I know you were such a troublemaker...
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: Hey! Wait for me!
attributes.name: Mio

attributes.content: Caught you!
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Keep your distance.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: That's no way to treat your old priest school classmate! I remember you were much more passionate back when we participated in debate competitions at Theros Temple.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Quiet down already. Loudmouth.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Looks like you know that I've come to debate you?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Don't worry, if you lose, I'll sing your defeat through a megaphone in standard hexameter.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Besides, you're the one who gave me the nickname 'Blaring Megaphone'. In return, I called you—
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Eloquent Rock! Blaring Megaphone!
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: So glad the two great Athenian debaters are here! We're saved!
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: Come with me! The apple farmers are making a scene in the temple, saying that the construction workers are delaying the quarrying to ruin this year's harvest by keeping the apples from sunlight.
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: What about Pallas? The people may have chosen to build the temple of their own accord, but she was the initial proponent. Why don't you talk to her to resolve your disputes?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Pallas has been crazy busy lately. No one's seen her since last night. Maybe someone else needed her.
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: When people heard about it, they were all excited to emulate the heroes in building the temple, doing as much or as little work anyone had time for.
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: Who could have thought that things would get so chaotic, even delaying the city's move to sunnier climes?
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: Please, find us a solution through debate... or at least find us a loser!
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: I guess the two of us in a room naturally drives people to think of debate...
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: That's why I said—keep your distance, and keep quiet.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: How was I supposed to know? Just because you're smart doesn't mean you should skip the explanation and go straight to the conclusion!
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Actually, no, that's not right. I DID come to debate you. Everything works out!
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I'm busy.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: Always the same excuse after all these years. How on Terra did you win that debate championship with such a bland tongue?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Thank you.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: You won't muddle your way out of this. For years, you've been running before I can finish the word 'debate'. Don't forget that there's still a competition we haven't settled!
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Come on, who are you arguing for? The fruit farmers or the temple workers?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: You've known me long enough to know that I don't lie.
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: *sigh*... I'm going to the new Oripathy clinic to get medicine. Can you move?
attributes.name: Lykeion

attributes.content: What...?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Oripathy medicine? Didn't he pick up some last Wednesday...? Is he getting worse?
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: I need to find out.
attributes.name: Hekádemos

attributes.content: Hey! Don't go!
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: The fruit farmers and our guys are about to come to blows! Who's gonna solve our problem? We can't just pull some random soul off the street!
attributes.name: Sober Craftsman

attributes.content: Look, Mio! So many people here in the temple! Looks like the Athenians are really excited to rebuild it! I feel like joining in myself!
attributes.name: Titi

attributes.content: Excuse me, sir. Are you looking for someone to help?
attributes.name: Titi
